content.problemStatement:
We call a binary array BBB of size MMM **winning** if it can be converted to the array [1,1,…,1][1, 1, \ldots, 1][1,1,…,1] by repeating the following operation a finite number of times:


Pick 1≤L≤R≤M1 \le L \le R \le M1≤L≤R≤M such that the number of 111's in the subarray B[L,R]B[L, R]B[L,R] is **strictly greater** than the number of 000's in it.

Then, replace BiB_iBi​ with 111 for all L≤i≤RL \le i \le RL≤i≤R.
Given an array AAA of size NNN, count the number of **winning** subarrays of AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN — the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers — A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of winning subarrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The following are the winning subarrays:

A[1,1]A[1, 1]A[1,1] : The array is [1][1][1]. All elements are already 111.
A[3,3]A[3, 3]A[3,3] : The array is [1][1][1]. All elements are already 111.
A[1,3]A[1, 3]A[1,3] : The array is [1,0,1][1, 0, 1][1,0,1]. We can do one operation with L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3 and transform the array into [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1].
